EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow moving cold front moved through southern New England and produced a long line of showers and thunderstorms that tracked east across the area.  Much of this line of storms was severe with damaging thunderstorm winds and occasional hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees on Route 47 in Hadley were downed by thunderstorm winds.  In Amherst, trees and wires on Mill Valley, Moody Bridge, and Chapel Roads and East Pleasant, Mechanic and East Maple Streets were downed by thunderstorm winds.  One of these trees was downed onto a car.  Two water treatment plants were also damaged in the resulting power loss in Amherst.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure tracked north of southern New England resulting in strong winds affecting portions of southern New England.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several Automated Surface Observing Stations across Barnstable County recorded wind gusts between 45 and 51 mph.  In Yarmouth, a tree was downed on Route 6A and Railroad Avenue.  Sustained winds at the same ASOS stations were between 31 and 37 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure tracked north of southern New England resulting in strong winds affecting portions of southern New England.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An amateur radio operator recorded a wind gust of 47 mph on their home weather station in Marion.  In Wareham, trees were downed on wires in two locations and in a third, wires were downed onto a car.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure tracked north of southern New England resulting in strong winds affecting portions of southern New England.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A handful of trees were downed in Montgomery.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through southern New England, bringing rain, a few thunderstorms, and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Automated Surface Observing Station at Blue Hill Observatory in East Milton recorded a wind gust of 62 mph.  In nearby Hingham, a tree on Winter Street was downed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through southern New England, bringing rain, a few thunderstorms, and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A few trees and wires were downed in Hatfield, Northampton, and Pelham.  In Ware, trees were downed blocking the intersection of Greenwich Road and Route 9.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through southern New England, bringing rain, a few thunderstorms, and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple trees, branches, and wires were downed across the Springfield metro area and in Palmer and Monson.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through southern New England, bringing rain, a few thunderstorms, and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell across Hampden County.  Up to two feet of water collected in several locations in Springfield and West Springfield, including Cambridge Street and Route 5.  Several cars flooded out.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved through southern New England, bringing rain, a few thunderstorms, and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds across portions of Hampshire Country resulted in a pole, a tree, and some wires down in Goshen and Worthington.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure tracked north of southern New England resulting in strong winds affecting portions of southern New England.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several wind gusts were measured on the island of Nantucket.  Two gusts were recorded by amateur radio operators on their home weather stations of 63 and 66 mph.  The Automated Surface Observing Station at Nantucket Memorial Airport recorded a wind gust of 54 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure tracked north of southern New England resulting in strong winds affecting portions of southern New England.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An amateur radio operator recorded a wind gust of 46 mph on their home weather station in Marblehead.  A tree on Beach Street and a tree on Tedesco Street in Marblehead were downed by strong winds onto wires.  In Danvers, a tree on Locust Street was downed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cool temperatures aloft, cyclonic flow, and an upper level jet combined to produce thunderstorms over western Massachusetts and northern Connecticut.

A strong upper level atmospheric disturbance interacted with a warm and moist airmass in place, leading to scattered strong to severe thunderstorms across western New England during the late afternoon and evening hours of Wednesday September 3rd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter size hail completely covered the ground along Surriner Road, approximately 1 mile southeast of Becket, during a thunderstorm. This was the second storm accompanied by large hail which occurred at this location on this day.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny size hail fell near East Otis during a thunderstorm.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large tree limbs, approximately 6 inches in diameter, were downed on East Windsor Road in East Windsor due to strong thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper level atmospheric disturbance interacted with a warm and moist airmass in place, leading to scattered strong to severe thunderstorms across western New England during the late afternoon and evening hours of Wednesday September 3rd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ping pong ball size hail fell along Surriner Road approximately 1 mile southeast of Becket during a thunderstorm. Shredded leaves were reported covering the road from the hail which occurred during this storm.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel to quarter size hail fell along Route 20 in Becket during a thunderstorm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Cool temperatures aloft, cyclonic flow, and an upper level jet combined to produce thunderstorms over western Massachusetts and northern Connecticut.
A cold front moving across southern New England combined with a low level jet to produce showers and thunderstorms.  Some of these storms became severe producing damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large branches and wires were downed by thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A shortwave trough moved eastward from the Great Lakes into Northern New England, eroding a mid level cap associated with a subtropical ridge over Southern New England.  This allowed widespread showers and thunderstorms to develop over the area resulting in numerous reports of wind damage throughout southern New England and the adjacent marine zones.  ||Despite the amount of damage the storms caused, they did bring some relief from a few days of record high temperatures across New England.

This allowed widespread showers and thunderstorms to develop over the area resulting in numerous reports of wind damage throughout southern New England and the adjacent marine zones.  ||Despite the amount of damage the storms caused, they did bring some relief from a few days of record high temperatures across New England.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees on Southville Road were downed by thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow moving cold front moved through southern New England and produced a long line of showers and thunderstorms that tracked east across the area.  Much of this line of storms was severe with damaging thunderstorm winds and occasional hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees and wires on Laurel Hill Road were downed by thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unstable atmosphere and plenty of sunshine led to widespread severe weather across Southern New England.  Cold upper level temperatures allowed large hail to form and form it did with one report of two inch diameter hail in Marlborough, Massachusetts.  There was heavy rain associated with some of the storms as well which resulted in some urban flooding and some flash flooding in Worcester, Massachusetts.

A slow moving cold front moved through southern New England and produced a long line of showers and thunderstorms that tracked east across the area.  Much of this line of storms was severe with damaging thunderstorm winds and occasional hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree on Interstate 91 near the Massachusetts Turnpike was downed by thunderstorm winds onto a car, puncturing the windshield.  A 61 year old man and a 57 year old woman were injured.  Rescuers had to cut through the tree before they could get to the people in the car.  The man was able to climb out by himself once the tree was out of the way, but the woman had to be extricated.  This also blocked both southbound lanes of Interstate 91 bringing traffic to a complete stop.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level shortwave combined with a weak pre-frontal trough to produce showers and thunderstorms across northern Massachusetts.  Most of the events that occurred were wind related with tree damage the main result.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Westhampton, trees on South Shore Road were downed by thunderstorm winds.  A trained spotter in Northampton estimated wind gusts of 60 mph and reported multiple trees downed by these winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved from west to east across eastern New York and western New England during Monday afternoon on June 16th. Numerous strong to severe thunderstorms developed with and preceding the front, many of which were accompanied by large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel to quarter size hail was reported in Leonx Dale during a thunderstorm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An approaching upper level trough combined with a moderately unstable atmosphere to produce showers and thunderstorms across southern New England.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several trees were downed by thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough moved across New England, combining with an unstable atmosphere to produce showers and thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny to quarter size hail fell in a swath from the Hyde Park area to Washington Street in Dedham.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Warm air advection occurred in a very moist atmosphere, resulting in extremely heavy rain in Easthampton and Holyoke in western Massachusetts.  Between three and four inches of rain fell in an hour to an hour and a half, rendering most streets impassable and stranding several cars.  The heavy rain was fairly isolated, with nearby communities receiving very little rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous roads in Holyoke were flooded, rendered impassable, and subsequently closed.  These included the following streets: Hampden, Northampton, Sargent, Howard, Senator, Beech, and Cherry Streets, Route 202 near the hospital, Southampton Road, and Ridgewood and Columbus Avenues.  The Holyoke fire department responded to 28 water related incidents.  Included in these incidents were: sinkholes and washouts on Ridgewood and Columbus Avenues and seven automobiles flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow moving cold front provided a focus for severe thunderstorms.  These thunderstorms produced very heavy rain that resulted in flash flooding, hail, and damaging winds.
A slow moving cold front provided a focus for severe thunderstorms.  These thunderstorms produced very heavy rain that resulted in flash flooding, hail, and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wires on Columbia Road and trees on Saywood Street were downed by thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved across Southern New England setting off isolated showers and thunderstorms that produced penny to nickel sized hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny size hail was spotted at the National Weather Service Office in Taunton, MA.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level disturbance moved across southern New England resulting in showers and thunderstorms.  Cold upper level temperatures created an unstable atmosphere and allowed large hail to form in these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Sudbury, Maynard Road was flooded with two feet of water stranding several cars.  Numerous other side roads also were flooded making them impassable.  A large number of basements and backyards were flooded with water accumulating up to several feet in the basements.  Four feet of water covered Main Street in Hudson, closing the road.  In Maynard, numerous basements and backyards were flooded with water accumulating several feet in the basements.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level shortwave combined with a weak pre-frontal trough to produce showers and thunderstorms across northern Massachusetts.  Most of the events that occurred were wind related with tree damage the main result.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees on Podunk Road were downed by thunderstorm winds.
